title: Surviving reach-in into system-spec-kit/scripts/node_modules contradicts cluster-B 'zero reach-ins' claim
why: Real cross-skill reach-in that cluster-B declared eliminated; non-fatal ONLY because loadStateSafety() wraps require() in try/catch (L129-137) and falls back to inline contract-equivalents, so it degrades silently rather than erroring.
fix: Resolve the tsx CJS register from deep-loop-runtime's own node_modules (or bare require under the runtime's resolution) and drop the system-spec-kit path.

title: Stale path-depth comment: says 'three levels up' but code uses four '..' segments
evidence: // Path depth from scripts/shared/ -> three levels up reaches .opencode/skills/   (code on L123 uses '..','..','..','..' = 4 segments)
why: Post-152 nesting added the deep-improvement/ level; real depth shared->scripts->deep-improvement->deep-loop-workflows->skills is four levels, code was bumped to 4 '..' but the comment still claims three.
fix: Change the comment to 'four levels up' to match the four '..' segments and the actual directory depth.

title: Seam guard walk scoped to deep-loop-runtime/{lib,scripts} only, structurally cannot catch sibling-skill reach-ins
evidence: for (const root of ['lib', 'scripts'] as const) { for (const file of walk(resolve(runtimeRoot, root))) {   (runtimeRoot = resolve(testDir,'..','..') = deep-loop-runtime root)
why: The REACH_IN_ARRAY regex on L72 ('system-spec-kit','scripts','node_modules') WOULD match reduce-state.cjs:123, but walk() never visits deep-loop-workflows or any sibling skill, so the guard's 'zero reach-ins' assertion is true-by-scope, not true-by-coverage.
fix: Widen the walk root to .opencode/skills (skipping each skill's node_modules, already excluded by walk L102) so cross-skill reach-ins are detected, not just runtime-local ones.

title: Additional uncovered cross-skill node_modules reach-ins in system-skill-advisor tsconfig (L9,13,14)
why: Build/type-resolution reach-ins into system-spec-kit/mcp_server/node_modules from a different skill — same coverage-hole class, missed by both cluster-B's deep-loop-runtime-scoped grep and the seam guard's walk.
fix: Point system-skill-advisor's typeRoots/paths at its own node_modules or vendor the type deps locally.

title: Doc-level reach-in in ai-council playbook; two other grep hits are self-referential (not reach-ins)
why: Lowest-risk instance (doc command, not runtime code) but still surfaced by the seam grep; the remaining two hits — INSTALL_GUIDE.md:985 and scripts/tests/test-folder-detector-functional.js:959 — live INSIDE system-spec-kit referencing its OWN node_modules, so they are NOT cross-skill reach-ins.
fix: Update the playbook command to a self-contained tsx loader path once the canonical runtime seam is settled.

title: Inertness hypothesis REFUTED — gate genuinely runs assertions on a cold clone
evidence: `npx --yes vitest@4.0.18 run ...` (no npm ci); config setupFiles -> ../../system-spec-kit/mcp_server/tests/_support/vitest-setup.ts imports only node:fs/os/path (lines 5-7); aliases.ts has zero imports; workspace-root.ts imports only node:fs/node:path; the 3 suites import only vitest+node-stdlib+those two local .ts; skill_advisor.py top imports (l23-32) are stdlib-only; node_modules git-ignored (.gitignore:98-99)
why: vitest/vite/esbuild come from the npx cache and every other import is stdlib or dependency-free local source, so config-load + setup + test imports + python dump all resolve with no node_modules — the assertions execute, not skip
fix: No fix needed for inertness; keep the dependency-free invariant (add a CI assertion that the 3 lib/test files import nothing from node_modules to prevent regression)

title: paths: trigger watches only mode-registry.json within deep-loop-workflows, not the rest of that skill
evidence: paths: lists '.opencode/skills/deep-loop-workflows/mode-registry.json' + 'system-skill-advisor/mcp_server/**' + parent-skill-check.cjs (l9-12); the 3 drift inputs (registry, skill_advisor.py, aliases.ts) are all inside these globs so registry<->advisor drift IS caught
why: if the Python router or any advisorRouting consumer reads a deep-loop-workflows file OTHER than mode-registry.json (unverified — not opened), a change there would not fire this gate; the gate's own three inputs are covered, this is a completeness edge not an inertness defect
fix: Broaden the deep-loop-workflows glob to the skill dir (or assert in-test that routing reads no deep-loop-workflows file besides mode-registry.json), and confirm no out-of-glob consumer participates in routing

title: Hypothesis refuted — shell-profile append is idempotent (grep-before-append guard)
evidence: if grep -q "export CHROME_PATH=" "$profile" 2>/dev/null; then log_warn ... return 0; npm -g gated by check_existing_installation (command -v bdg, L219) at L328
why: Second run cannot duplicate the CHROME_PATH line and will not re-run npm install -g; the claimed duplication risk does not exist.
fix: None — adversarial check clears the P0/P1 shell-profile duplication hypothesis for this installer.

title: Hypothesis refuted — cupt install guarded, zero shell-profile writes
evidence: L5 header: 'Does NOT modify opencode.json or any config files — prints snippets only'; L105 install_cupt: 'if [[ "$CUPT_ALREADY_INSTALLED" == "true" ]]; then return 0' (set by command -v cupt at L90).
why: pipx/pip install is gated by command -v cupt and there is no profile-append code path, so re-runs cannot duplicate or reinstall.
fix: None — this installer has no global shell-state mutation to make idempotent.

title: mcp_subroutes install gate3_location names config files the workflow does not edit
evidence: gate3_location: 'MCP server install dirs + runtime config (.opencode.json / .claude/settings.json / .codex/config.toml)' vs doctor_mcp_install.yaml config_files opencode 'opencode.json' (L224) and claude '.claude/mcp.json' (L230).
why: Schema (L15) calls gate3_location the 'concrete mutation target', but it cites .claude/settings.json and .opencode.json — neither is what step_4_configure actually edits, so the Gate-3 target is misdeclared.
fix: Align gate3_location to opencode.json + .claude/mcp.json + .codex/config.toml + .vscode/mcp.json to match config_files.

title: standalone /doctor:update declared 'mutates' but omits gate3_location
evidence: standalone: - command: /doctor:update ... mutating: mutates (L226) with no gate3_location field, unlike mcp_subroutes install (L199) and debug (L209) which both carry it.
why: Breaks the per-route schema (header L15) and the mutates→gate3_location convention; the manifest exposes no concrete mutation target for the one orchestrator that rebuilds all DBs.
fix: Add gate3_location (spec-kit memory DB + embeddings/causal index dirs) to the standalone /doctor:update row.

title: cli_skill_diagnostics is reference-only — server-install loop cannot npm-install a CLI skill
evidence: step_3_install iterates install_plan from servers: map + --server valid_values [mk-spec-memory, mk_skill_advisor, mk_code_index, code_mode, sequential_thinking] (L83); cli_skill_diagnostics (mcp-figma/open-design/chrome-devtools/click-up, L193-217) appear in neither, and no workflow step references the block.
why: The additive block has no executed install_steps and is excluded from server filtering, so it can never accidentally trigger an install from the loop.
fix: None — confirms Task 3 safety; optionally annotate the block as documentation-only for future maintainers.

note: all 23 relative requires/imports into deep-loop-runtime/system-spec-kit from deep-loop-workflows resolve (accounting for vitest .js->.ts); no off-by-one broken requires remain
